targetTruthTable.desc: B inhibits A: only Out1 active
A inhibits B: only Out0 active

hint: Out0 = A AND NOT(B): invert B, then use a transistor with ctrl=A and data=NOT_B. Out1 = B AND NOT(A): invert A, then use a transistor with ctrl=B and data=NOT_A.
title: Dual Inhibit Gate
objective: Build two inhibit outputs: A·B̄ and B·Ā
description: The inhibit (AND-NOT) function outputs HIGH only when one input is HIGH and the other is LOW. Build both directions simultaneously: Out0 = A AND NOT(B), Out1 = B AND NOT(A). This is the core of half-subtractor borrow logic and priority arbitration circuits.
